top_signals.title: India’s Fuel Consumption Slips Despite Record Crude Oil Imports
top_signals.summary: Despite recovering energy flows out of the Middle East, India’s fuel consumption last month fell by 3.7% from May to a total of 19.24 million metric tons, Reuters has reported, citing data from the country’s oil ministry. Fuel consumption was also down on the year in June, by 3.1%, the data showed. However, gasoline sales were up on the year, by a substantial 7.4%, even though they were down on the month, by 3.2%. Reuters suggested sales of gasoline to Russia played a role in the sales increase, as Ukrainian drone attacks on Russian…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Indonesia drops KF-21 co-production plans, eying stock warplanes instead
top_signals.summary: Officials in Jakarta are also considering fighter options other than the South Korean-made jets.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: BONK faces $20 million treasury drain after attacker spends $4 million to pass malicious proposal
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Google and RWE Back German Nuclear Startup Proxima Fusion at €2.4 Billion Valuation
top_signals.summary: German startup Proxima Fusion has raised €411 million ($469 million) from a range of investors, including national energy firm RWE AG and Alphabet Inc.’s Google, to develop a nuclear fusion plant it hopes will be operational in the 2030s.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
